item.title: Compilers Resources
item.description:
This post is a compilation of great resources I found while building a type checker for Python. These resources are free and highly focused on specific topics, making them ideal for learning by doing rather than going through extensive materials.

Parser

There are different ways to approach parsing. You can either write one by hand or use a parser generator. For compilers or interpreters, you can use a parser generator. However, if you’re working on tools like formatters or language servers, your parser needs to handle broken code gracefully. This can be either done with a tool like treesitter that can handle broken code to some extent and also by writing your own. Of course writing your own is more fun.
